business.headline: July 31 vs August 31: Which ITR filing deadline applies to you?The countdown to ITR filing has begun, but this year there's a catch. The familiar one-deadline-for-all approach is gone. Whether you need to file by July 31 or August 31 depends on your taxpayer category and the ITR form you are using.

Markets snap 4-day rally as profit booking offsets IT surge; Trent down 12%Benchmark indices ended slightly lower on Tuesday as profit booking offset strong gains in IT shares. Caution ahead of the US Fed minutes and the earnings season kept sentiment measured, even as the rupee strengthened.

Trent loses Rs 13,000 crore in market value: Why are investors selling?The stock fell as much as 12.33% to Rs 2,931.15 during the day's trade, making it the biggest loser on the Nifty 50 index. By around 1:15 pm, Trent's market capitalisation had dropped to about Rs 97,311 crore from Rs 1.11 lakh crore in the previous session, eroding nearly Rs 13,356 crore in investor wealth in a single day.

Multiple marriages to family businesses: India's new need for wealth managersBuilding wealth is only half the journey. As Indian families become more complex, protecting that wealth and passing it on smoothly is becoming an even bigger challenge.

Government denies reports of E25 petrol rollout, says no decision taken yetGovernment sources have denied reports of an E25 rollout and said no decision has been taken yet. They said E20 has been in safe use after testing and any further move will depend on scientific findings.

Big Tech's India problem: Why the government is tightening the screwsIndia has stepped up scrutiny of Google, Meta, Telegram and Signal over platform design, safety and data issues. The actions signal a broader shift towards home-grown digital rules centred on accountability and user protection.
South Korea's Kospi suffers 8% crash as tech giants come under pressureSouth Korea's benchmark Kospi index dropped more than 8%, ending at 7,392.04 after a broad sell-off in technology stocks.

EPFO announces 8.25% PF interest for FY26. Check new rules you need to knowThe EPFO has issued an official circular directing all its field offices to begin crediting interest at 8.25% on provident fund deposits for FY26.

Centre weighs phased E25 petrol rollout by 2029 amid E20 fuel debateWith E20 petrol now available nationwide, the Centre is evaluating E21 by 2027 and E25 by 2029. Officials say any move to higher ethanol blends will be phased to address industry readiness and consumer concerns.
Sensex, Nifty gain for fifth day as IT stocks leadThe BSE Sensex rose 89.69 points, or 0.11%, to 78,374.76, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 38.90 points, or 0.16%, to trade at 24,469.25 as of 9:45 am.

Saudi Arabia announces biggest crude price cut in over 20 years for AsiaSaudi Aramco sharply cut its August Arab Light price for Asia after oil prices eased and supplies rose. The move signals a market shifting from disruption fears to stronger competition for buyers.

AI may not be Indian IT's biggest threat after all. Here's whyMorgan Stanley has said AI fears may have led investors to misread India's IT sector. It said Indian IT firms could gain as companies deploy AI across their operations.
Sensex, Nifty today: Will markets continue to rise or fall?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,546 at 7:53 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open above Monday's closing level of 24,430.35.

Mandatory PF contribution stays at Rs 1,800. Will your take-home salary rise?EPFO has clarified that mandatory EPF contributions will remain capped at Rs 1,800 a month under the statutory wage ceiling. The move gives employees and employers greater clarity, but leaves workers to balance higher in-hand pay against retirement savings.

Microsoft to cut 4,800 jobs as AI spending continues to riseThe latest round of layoffs comes at a time when major technology companies are spending billions of dollars on AI infrastructure and facing growing pressure to show returns on those investments.

EPF scheme 2026: Can employers refuse to match higher PF contributions?The new EPF Scheme 2026 has clarified the rules on voluntary PF contributions, but what does that mean for salaried employees? Can your employer say no to matching the extra contribution? Have a look.

Vietnam and 5 others climb World Bank's income ladder. Where does India stand?This year, no country moved to a lower category. Instead, six countries improved their position. Jordan, Micronesia, the Philippines, Sri Lanka and Vietnam moved from the lower-middle-income group to the upper-middle-income category.

Sensex ends 521 points higher, Nifty above 24,400; HDFC up 3%The BSE Sensex rose 521.16 points, or 0.67%, to close at 78,285.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 159.50 points, or 0.66%, to settle at 24,430.35.

Sebi plans to make it easier to short stocks. Here's what it means for investorsSebi is considering wider access to stock borrowing and lower collateral for short selling. The move is aimed at shifting activity towards cash equities as derivative losses mount for retail investors.

HDFC, RBL, ICICI jump up to 4%: Why are banking stocks rising today?HDFC Bank led a broad rally in banking stocks after reporting strong provisional June quarter business growth. The move lifted expectations for a solid earnings season even as analysts tracked loan mix, CASA pressure and improving macro signals.

entertainment.headline: Why movie tickets suddenly cost more: Truth behind dynamic pricing at multiplexes Multiplexes are using dynamic pricing to change ticket rates based on demand and timing. The system is drawing scrutiny because many cinemagoers notice higher prices without understanding why.

Karavali trailer: Prajwal-Raj B Shetty film unpacks Kambala, pride and politics Prajwal Devaraj and Raj B Shetty's long-awaited Karavali unveils an action-packed trailer rooted in coastal Karnataka, with Kambala (Buffalo racing), mystery and scale taking centre stage.

That lil baby bump: Fans shower love on Deepika Padukone's new advertisement Actor Deepika Padukone's latest advertisement has grabbed attention online, with fans praising her baby bump and pregnancy glow. The actor reportedly shot the campaign while she was four months pregnant.

Jasbir Jassi recalls meeting cop KPS Gill as Satluj discusses Punjab Police's legacy Singer and actor Jasbir Jassi backed Diljit Dosanjh over Satluj's removal and recalled being made to sing before KPS Gill. His account has added another personal recollection to the renewed debate over Punjab's militancy years.

Prajakta-Rohit kick off Mismatched 4 shooting: All about the final chapter Netflix started filming Mismatched season 4 with actors Prajakta Koli and Rohit Saraf on Tuesday. The final season will pick up after Dimple and Rishi paused their engagement to focus on their careers.

Dilip Kumar left me a treasury of memories: Saira Banu on his death anniversary Veteran actor Saira Banu remembered late legendary star Dilip Kumar on his fifth death anniversary with an emotional note and photos from their life together. She called him her "Sahib" and reflected on their memories, companionship and the love they shared.

Satluj scene featuring Arjun Rampal's 'genocide' monologue gets internet talking A scene from Satluj featuring Arjun Rampal as a CBI officer has gone viral online. The monologue has intensified questions around the film's OTT removal and the wider debate on censorship.

Ahmed Khan: Social media can make or break a film before it's even over Welcome to the Jungle director Ahmed Khan said instant social media reactions can shape a film's fate before it ends. He said word of mouth still matters most, citing the Akshay Kumar starrer's rising response.

Hema Malini recalls Dharmendra's last advice; calls Sunny, Bobby wonderful boys Hema Malini has shared the final advice Dharmendra gave the family before his death, saying he wanted them to always stay united. She also opened up about her close bond with Sunny and Bobby Deol, dismissing rumours of a family rift.

Karan Johar to distribute Ramayana in India as Dharma secures Rs 259 crore deal Dharma Productions has acquired the India theatrical distribution rights of Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana after negotiations with the co-producers. The Rs 250-crore deal underlines the scale of the two-part epic and the industry attention around it.

All of us should thank you for Satluj: RGV praises Honey Trehan amid OTT takedown Filmmaker Ram Gopal Varma praised Satluj and said audiences should thank director Honey Trehan for making such a powerful and eye-opening film. The exchange came as Trehan responded to his earlier post praising the Diljit Dosanjh-starrer.

Is Lokesh Cinematic Universe nearing its end? Kaithi, Vikram director breaks silence Director Lokesh Kanagaraj has dismissed speculation that the Lokesh Cinematic Universe (LCU) will end after Benz. He said Kaithi 2, Vikram 2, Rolex and Benz remain part of his plans despite his other commitments.

Pinaka teaser review: Ganesh's Kannada film has big ambition, bigger red flags The teaser of Kannada actor Ganesh's mythology fantasy, Pinaka, unveils a world of sacred symbols and war. It draws curiosity for its concept, but its CGI-heavy execution raises doubts about the film's scale.

Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj had multiple title changes. Here's all there is to know Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj saw multiple changes in title. The original name was changed to Punjab 95 around the time the makers submitted the film for censor clearance.

Rajkumar Hirani says Ranbir Kapoor may have influenced son Vir to become an actor Filmmaker Rajkumar Hirani revealed that working with Ranbir Kapoor during Sanju may have inspired his son Vir Hirani to become an actor instead of a director. The filmmaker also shared how he initially tried to discourage Vir before backing his acting debut in Pritam and Pedro.

Sundeep Kishan says South cinema wasn't just underestimated, it wasn't respected Promoting his Netflix series, Super Subbu, actor Sundeep Kishan reflected on how South cinema was once viewed in Hindi film circles. He said wider access and changing perceptions have finally brought overdue respect to the industry.

Satluj to Jana Nayagan: 6 films that fought censorship battles over political rows Political themes have often landed films in trouble with the censor board and various authorities. From Satluj to Jana Nayagan, these six films sparked debates that went far beyond the big screen.

Kriti Sanon reveals freezing her eggs during Mimi prep, calls it the best gift Actor Kriti Sanon said she froze her eggs while preparing for Mimi. She described it as a smart personal decision and even spoke about the changes her body went through the entire process.

Sanjay Dutt visits Hyderabad cancer hospital, says courage is key in fight Actor Sanjay Dutt met cancer patients at actor-politician Balakrishna's Basavatarakam hospital and shared encouragement from his own recovery. He talked about how one should have courage and mental strength to deal with cancer.

general news.headline: ARG 0-1 EGY Live: Can Messi make amends after his penalty miss?Argentina (ARG) vs Egypt (EGY) Live, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: (ARG 0-1 EGY) Argentina have 45 minutes to keep their World Cup dream alive after a frustrating first half in Atlanta. Egypt, meanwhile, are just one strong defensive effort away from a famous upset as Hamdy Fathy joins the action after the break.

3 tankers hit in latest attacks in Hormuz within 24 hours amid US-Iran tensionsThe latest incident followed Qatar's accusation that Iran attacked its LNG carrier, Al Rekayyat, while it was sailing through the Strait of Hormuz overnight.

US left with only nuke option: Tehran Deputy Mayor warns Trump over attacking IranResponding to a question on whether there were concerns about Israel or the US targeting the funeral procession, Hamidreza Gholamzadeh claimed both countries had considered disrupting the event but ultimately refrained.

The daring story behind PM Modi's praise for Biju Patnaik in IndonesiaToday, many remember Biju Patnaik as the former Chief Minister of Odisha. But before that, Patnaik was an ace pilot who played crucial roles during the Indian and Indonesian freedom struggles. Read on to find out why PM Modi praised Patnaik in Indonesia.

Watch: Road caves in on highway in Gurugram, triggers 10-km traffic jamA section of NH-48 caved in near Sunbeam company in Gurugram's Narsinghpur area. The collapse triggered a long jam from IFFCO Chowk to Narsinghpur and prompted a diversion advisory.

E20 can damage rubber parts in older cars, says ARAI report that wasn't made publicThe use of E20 petrol in E10-compatible vehicles can damage fuel-system rubber parts, found a study by the Automotive Research Association of India (ARAI). The report, which was not made public, however, backed the use of E20 fuel. The findings align with the concerns expressed by owners of pre-2023 vehicles.

No new Thars on rent in Goa now? State plans curbs after spate of accidentsFollowing a series of fatal accidents involving rented Mahaindra Thar SUVs, the Goa government is planning to stop issuing new rental permits for the vehicle. Goa Transport Minister Mauvin Godinho said that Thar has a high speed and lacks driver control.

I like her, but she made mistake: Trump on Meloni's stance on IranDonald Trump said Giorgia Meloni's refusal to support Washington during the Iran conflict had damaged their relationship. The dispute has exposed wider policy differences between Rome and Washington ahead of a tense NATO summit meeting.

Video: Heavy rain hits Maharashtra, commuters trek home on flooded tracks in PalgharFlooded roads, stalled trains, tractors replacing transport and commuters trekking along railway tracks - relentless rain has brought parts of Palghar to a standstill, leaving thousands struggling to get home.

Were Siya and Chetan secretly married before Ketan Agarwal's murder? Cops probeInvestigators have uncovered fresh digital evidence that could change the direction of the Pune realtor murder case, with chats pointing to a possible secret marriage between the two accused and a deeper conspiracy.

Is 'wildflowering' the right way to date?It's got nothing to do with gardening, picking flowers together or exploring the wild. Hint: It's a low-key, refreshing term in the otherwise "new toxic dating trend" buzz. Read on to find out the good, and the less good part about it.

Anshula Kapoor-Rohan Thakkar Wedding Reception LIVEEntertainment News Today Live Updates: Welcome to the world of entertainment. We help you with all the latest happenings from the world of films, OTT, television and celebrity buzz. Stay tuned throughout the day as we bring you breaking news, exclusive interviews, movie announcements, box office updates and more.

Quarterfinals live: Djokovic in action after Zverev reaches quartersWimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic and Felix Auger-Aliassime are in action in the men's quarterfinal match. Earlier, defending champion Jannik Sinner beat Germany's Jan-Lennard Struff in three sets.

Ram Temple donation row: Champat Rai breaks silence, questions role of Anil MishraIn his first response to the SIT after stepping down, Champat Rai has questioned the bank's role in the Ram Temple cash-counting process and alleged that mandatory security protocols were ignored.

Khamenei funeral: Iran thanks India, says US threats made 13 countries stay awayIran thanked India for attending Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's state funeral in Tehran. The message came even as Iran's semi-official Tasnim News Agency said that US pressure led at least 13 countries, including two Gulf nations, to scale back or cancel participation.

Caught on dashcam: Men swarm car, attack family on expressway; 3 arrestedA family was attacked after a group of men intercepted their car on the Bengaluru–Mysuru Expressway. A dashcam video of the assault has gone viral as police are yet to announce any action.

How Bangladesh, China are putting India to a big Teesta testDuring Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman's China visit, Beijing agreed to accelerate a feasibility study for the Teesta river project near India's Siliguri Corridor. China had been angling for it for years but was held back by the Sheikh Hasina government's caution. That's no longer the case. This is why India will be looking closely at this development.

Video: Miraculous escape for bystanders as landslide sweeps fuel tanker in WayanadWayanad: The landslide, which struck near Meenakshi Bridge following incessant rainfall, has claimed at least three lives, injured eight people and left seven workers missing, officials said.

Vijay govt hikes TASMAC staff salaries by 25%, first revision in 20 yearsThe Tamil Nadu government has announced a major pay hike for TASMAC employees, linking it directly to efforts to curb corruption and overcharging. But will the move end complaints from liquor buyers?

Watch: Twin tornadoes dance over central China, kill at least 11Two tornadoes ripped through China's Hubei province, killing 11 people and injuring over 330. Here's what caused the rare and destructive weather event.

Brace for heavy rain: Monsoon to drench Northwest, East India on WednesdayIMD has forecast widespread rain across India as the southwest monsoon continues to advance, getting closer to covering the entire country.

3 dead after landslide hits construction site in Wayanad, several feared trappedA major landslide struck near a tunnel project site in Kerala's Wayanad, with around seven people believed to be trapped. The incident, which occurred near Mundakkai, has revived memories of the 2024 disaster.

Ahmed Khan: Social media can make or break a film before it's even overWelcome to the Jungle director Ahmed Khan said instant social media reactions can shape a film's fate before it ends. He said word of mouth still matters most, citing the Akshay Kumar starrer's rising response.

sports.headline: Wimbledon: Jannik Sinner digs deep to reach semis, outsmarts big-serving Struff Jannik Sinner beat Jan-Lennard Struff in straight sets and Coco Gauff came from a set down to defeat Jessica Pegula and reach the Wimbledon semi-finals. Struff earned a standing ovation for his landmark run, while Gauff broke new ground with her first last-four appearance here.

ARG 0-1 EGY Live: Can Messi make amends after his penalty miss? Argentina (ARG) vs Egypt (EGY) Live, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: (ARG 0-1 EGY) Argentina have 45 minutes to keep their World Cup dream alive after a frustrating first half in Atlanta. Egypt, meanwhile, are just one strong defensive effort away from a famous upset as Hamdy Fathy joins the action after the break.

Days after viral prank, Djokovic reunites with ball girl, gifts her signed cap Novak Djokovic turned a viral Wimbledon prank into a heartwarming moment by reuniting with the ball girl he had jokingly startled during his second-round match. The Serbian apologised with a smile, gifted her a signed Wimbledon cap, and shared a light-hearted exchange that has won over tennis fans.

Quarterfinals live: Djokovic in action after Zverev reaches semis Wimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic and Felix Auger-Aliassime are in action in the men's quarterfinal match. Earlier, defending champion Jannik Sinner beat Germany's Jan-Lennard Struff in three sets.

Happy Birthday captain cool: Thalapathy Vijay's heartfelt wish for Thala Dhoni Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Joseph Vijay wished MS Dhoni on his birthday and praised his leadership, humility and bond with the state. The tribute underlined how deeply the former India captain remains woven into Tamil Nadu's sporting identity.

Shubman Gill plays cricket with Antonelli in Mercedes garage at British GP. Video Shubman Gill played corridor cricket with Kimi Antonelli inside the Mercedes garage at Silverstone. The crossover video linked cricket and Formula One before Gill's ODI tour of England.

Shapoor Zadran, former Afghanistan fast bowler, dies at 38 after prolonged illness Former Afghanistan fast bowler Shapoor Zadran died at 38 in New Delhi following a prolonged battle with a rare immune disorder, leaving the global cricketing fraternity mourning one of the pioneers of Afghanistan cricket.

Messi, Salah set for World Cup showdown as Argentina face Egypt Lionel Messi and Mohamed Salah will go head-to-head on Tuesday when Argentina and Egypt meet in the Round of 16, and the World Cup has handed us exactly the kind of match it needed. A place in the quarter-finals is on the line. So is a piece of individual history.

Ronaldo, you'll always be my inspiration: Siraj pens emotional note FIFA World Cup 2026: Mohammed Siraj posted an emotional tribute to Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's World Cup exit against Spain. The message captured how Ronaldo's reach extends beyond football, even as debate over his unfinished World Cup story continued.

You despise me because I am a woman: Paraguay senator demands Mbappe apology FIFA World Cup 2026: Paraguay senator Celeste Amarilla has demanded an apology from Kylian Mbappé after their World Cup-linked social media clash. The row now sits at the junction of racism, politics and gender.

Suryakumar calls out fake statement on captaincy snub, says happy for Team IndiaDismissing viral social media fabrications about his exclusion, a sidelined Suryakumar Yadav has thrown his full support behind Shreyas Iyer's transitioning T20 unit ahead of India's must-win clash in Nottingham.

Cristiano Ronaldo never lifted the World Cup. He lifted PortugalCristiano Ronaldo never conquered the World Cup, but he transformed Portugal forever, turning a nation of hopeful challengers into perennial contenders and leaving behind a legacy that extends far beyond trophies.

India hope for Sooryavanshi storm as Shreyas looks to break duck in NottinghamEngland vs India, 3rd T20I preview: With India trailing 1-0 and reeling from a rare three-match losing streak, new skipper Shreyas Iyer faces a trial by fire in Nottingham as he searches for his first win at the helm.

FIFA president condemns racist abuse of Kylian Mbappe after Paraguay rowFIFA president Gianni Infantino has condemned the racist comments directed at France captain Kylian Mbappe by a Paraguayan senator, saying the football community stands in solidarity with the French star.

Sarvesh Kushare, the understated ascent, and the science of mature longevityFollowing his historic 2.31m national record, understated Indian high jumper Sarvesh Kushare is quietly defying the biological clock, proving that technical mastery and mature longevity can carry him to the cusp of global podium finishes.

Wimbledon order of play: Djokovic, Sinner headline quarter-final actionWimbledon's quarter-final action on Tuesday features Novak Djokovic, Jannik Sinner, Naomi Osaka and Coco Gauff. Djokovic and defending champion Sinner are on course for a blockbuster semi-final showdown if both come through their respective last-eight clashes.

Cristiano Ronaldo says Euro as significant as World Cup, divides social mediaCristiano Ronaldo said Portugal's Euro 2016 triumph matters to him as much as a World Cup. The remark followed Portugal's exit in the Round of 16 stage on Monday and sparked a divided reaction over his international legacy.

Pochettino refuses to blame Balogun's red card controversy for USA's exitUnited States coach Mauricio Pochettino admitted he was "disappointed" and "frustrated" by the backlash over Folarin Balogun's controversial availability but insisted it played no part in his side's 4-1 World Cup defeat to Belgium on Monday.

Overturn this: Belgium troll USA, FIFA and Trump in a crisp Instagram postFIFA World Cup 2026: Belgium's clinical 4-1 victory over the United States sparked a fierce off-pitch row as the Red Devils mocked Donald Trump and FIFA over the controversial overturning of Folarin Balogun's suspension.

Belgium bury the Balogun circus and USA's World Cup dream in 4-1 winBelgium produced their best display of the tournament to thrash co-hosts USA 4-1 in Seattle. Charles De Ketelaere starred with a brace as Rudi Garcia's bold selection calls paid off, while Folarin Balogun failed to make an impact on his return.

Trump-backed Balogun fails to justify dramatic FIFA reprieve against BelgiumFolarin Balogun's controversial return took centre stage before kick-off, but the United States striker was unable to justify the pre-match attention as Belgium thrashed the co-hosts 4-1 to reach the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals on Monday.

Watch: Yamal consoles tearful Ronaldo after Spain knock Portugal out of World CupCristiano Ronaldo's World Cup dream came to a heartbreaking end on Monday as Portugal suffered a Round of 16 defeat to Spain. Moments after the final whistle, Spain teenager Lamine Yamal put celebrations on hold to console the tearful Portugal captain in a gesture that quickly captured attention.

Mbappe slams Paraguayan senator's racist attack over World Cup defeatKylian Mbappe condemned Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla after her racist social media post following France's win over Paraguay. France's football authorities and Paraguay's government rejected the remarks, with the FFF preparing legal action.

Ronaldo leaves Portugal future undecided, confirms he played his final World CupCristiano Ronaldo confirmed Spain's dramatic Round of 16 defeat marked his final FIFA World Cup but stopped short of announcing his retirement from Portugal duty. The 41-year-old said he would take time to reflect before deciding on his international future.

Roger Federer steals Wimbledon spotlight by staying in empty Royal BoxRoger Federer became an unlikely Wimbledon talking point after staying in the Royal Box long after most guests had left. The eight-time champion won admiration simply for watching every minute of Centre Court action.

USA 1-4 BEL Highlights: Belgium end American World Cup dreamUSA vs Belgium Highlights, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: (USA 1-4 BEL) Belgium produce a dominant display to beat the United States 4-1 and book their place in the FIFA World Cup quarterfinals. After taking control early, the Red Devils never looked back, comfortably seeing out the contest to set up a last-eight clash with Spain.

Roberto Martinez steps down as Portugal boss after World Cup exit to SpainFIFA World Cup 2026: Roberto Martinez stepped down as Portugal head coach after the 1-0 Round of 16 defeat to Spain. The exit ended his contract and may also mark Cristiano Ronaldo's final international appearance.

Cristiano Ronaldo bows out as Spain end Portugal's World Cup dreamCristiano Ronaldo leaves the World Cup without the one prize that always eluded him, but never without the admiration of the game he helped define. Portugal's future may finally be ready to move beyond him, yet their greatest footballing story will forever begin with the man who carried the nation for more than two decades.

Ronaldo holds back tears as his last World Cup ends in heartbreak: VideoCristiano Ronaldo's World Cup dream ended in heartbreak as Portugal crashed out to Spain in the Round of 16. The veteran forward fought back tears after what is expected to be his final World Cup, with football's biggest prize set to remain beyond his reach.

POR 0-1 ESP Highlights: Spain send Ronaldo, Portugal packing in R16Portugal (POR) vs Spain (ESP) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: (POR 0-1 ESP): Spain are through to the World Cup quarter-finals after Mikel Merino's late winner. Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career ends as Portugal fall in Dallas.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
